content.problemStatement:
For an array AAA of size NNN, we define f(A)f(A)f(A) in the following way:


Start with score=0score = 0score=0, and your hand is empty.
For each day i=1,2,…,Ni = 1, 2, \ldots, Ni=1,2,…,N, repeat the following steps in **this order**:

If your hand is empty, you may choose to pick up the current item with worth AiA_iAi​, or you may choose not to.
If your hand contains an item, you may choose to sell the item and add it's worth to scorescorescore (or again, you may choose not to).
If your hand contains an item till now, the item's worth increases by 111 due to interest.


Your hand should be empty at the end of the NNN steps. Further, note that it is impossible for your hand to contain 222 items at the same time.
f(A)f(A)f(A) is the maximum possible scorescorescore possible, i.e. sum of worth of sold items.
Given an array AAA of length NNN, find

∑L=1N∑R=LNf(A[L,R])\sum_{L = 1}^{N} \sum_{R = L}^{N} f(A[L, R])∑L=1N​∑R=LN​f(A[L,R])

where A[L,R]A[L, R]A[L,R] denotes the subarray [AL,AL+1,AL+2,…,AR][A_L, A_{L + 1}, A_{L + 2}, \ldots, A_R][AL​,AL+1​,AL+2​,…,AR​]. Since the answer may be large, find it modulo 998244353998244353998244353.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of multiple lines of input.

The first line contains a single integer NNN - the length of the array.
The second line contains NNN integers - A1,A2,…,ANA_1, A_2, \ldots, A_NA1​,A2​,…,AN​.




Output Format
For each test case, output ∑L=1N∑R=LNf(A[L,R])\sum_{L = 1}^{N} \sum_{R = L}^{N} f(A[L, R])∑L=1N​∑R=LN​f(A[L,R]) modulo 998244353998244353998244353.
constraints: The sum of NNN over all test cases does not exceed 2⋅1052 \cdot 10^52⋅105.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test case 1 : The values of fff for the subarrays are as follows:

L=1,R=1L = 1, R = 1L=1,R=1 : We want f([10])f([10])f([10]). We can choose to pick up the item on day 111 and sell it as well. This gets a score of 101010.
L=1,R=2L = 1, R = 2L=1,R=2 : We want f([10,0])f([10, 0])f([10,0]). We can choose to pick up the item on day 111, get interest on day 111, and sell it on day 222. This gets a score of 111111.
L=2,R=2L = 2, R = 2L=2,R=2 : We want f([0])f([0])f([0]). Choose to do nothing. Score is 000.
